io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.DtrTest#pro.description:
Inspects the test method for doc annotations and emits the corresponding render-machine calls. All five annotation types are optional and independent. They are always emitted in this fixed order regardless of the order they appear in source code:
  1. {@link DocSection} — section heading via {@code sayNextSection()}
  2. {@link DocDescription} — narrative paragraphs via {@code say()}
  3. {@link DocNote} — info callout boxes via {@code sayRaw()}
  4. {@link DocWarning} — warning callout boxes via {@code sayRaw()}
  5. {@link DocCode} — HTML-escaped {@code 
    } blocks via {@code sayRaw()}

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.rendermachi.description: LaTeX document class (e.g., "article", "report", custom).

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.rendermachi.description:
Documents the current call site using {@link StackWalker}.

Blue ocean innovation: the documentation knows exactly where it was written. Every section generated by this call carries provenance — class, method, line number — derived from the live JVM stack at the moment of invocation.

Uses {@code StackWalker.getInstance(RETAIN_CLASS_REFERENCE)} to walk the call stack and find the first frame outside of DTR's own machinery.

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.render.Rend.description: Select LaTeX template based on system property (uses cached instances for JEP 526). The -Ddtr.latex.format property controls which academic/patent format: - arxiv (default): arXiv pre-print submissions - patent: USPTO patent exhibit format - ieee: IEEE journal articles - acm: ACM conference proceedings - nature: Nature scientific reports

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.rendermachi.description: Creates an ACM template with default SIGPLAN template and no conference header.

Dispatches an action to all contained render machines concurrently using structured concurrency.

Java 26 Enhancement (JEP 492 - Structured Concurrency):

  1. Creates a {@link StructuredTaskScope} scope
  2. Forks one task per machine — each task calls the action on its machine via a virtual thread
  3. Joins when all tasks complete (blocks until all finish or an exception is thrown)
  4. Propagates exceptions with full structured context

This replaces the previous manual future-waiting pattern with JVM-native semantics. StructuredTaskScope ensures:

  • No error swallowing: If any renderer fails, the failure is immediate
  • Automatic cleanup: All tasks are guaranteed to complete before method returns
  • Structured lifetime: Try-with-resources ensures proper scope closure
  • Zero boilerplate: Simple, declarative concurrency patterns

params.name: action
params.description: the operation to invoke on each render machine

throws.description: if any machine fails (wraps first exception)

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.rendermachi.description:
Documents an Erlang/OTP-style supervision tree showing which supervisors manage which workers. Renders as a Mermaid {@code graph TD} with supervisor → child edges, restart strategies, and worker counts.

Blue ocean: surfaces fault-tolerance topology that is implicit in OTP app files but never visualised in documentation.

params.name: supervisors
params.description: map of supervisor name → list of child names

Documents a method's structure using Project Babylon CodeReflection API.

On Java 26+, uses {@code java.lang.reflect.code.CodeReflection.reflect(method)} to introspect the method's bytecode operations — control flow, method calls, field accesses, etc. Renders a detailed breakdown of operation types and their counts.

On Java 26 and earlier, gracefully falls back to rendering the method signature (parameters with types, return type) extracted via reflection.

Example:

{@code sayCodeModel(SayEvent.class.getMethod("toString")); // Java 26+: Renders operation breakdown (INVOKE: 3, FIELD_READ: 1, etc.) // Java 26-: Renders String toString() signature only }

params.name: method
params.description: the method to introspect and document (must not be null)

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.rendermachi.description: Decision tree flowchart — no-op in base class. */

Documents a logical property by evaluating a predicate against a list of sample inputs. Renders a table showing each input, the predicate result, and a PASS/FAIL marker. Fails the test if any input violates the property.

Blue ocean: property-based invariant documentation — proves correctness across representative examples inline with the narrative.

params.name: property
params.description: description of the invariant (e.g. "result is always positive")

params.name: check
params.description: predicate that must hold for every input

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.rendermachi.description:
Documents a decision algorithm as a Mermaid {@code flowchart TD}. The {@code branches} map encodes the tree: keys are node labels (questions / conditions); values are either a {@code String} (leaf answer) or a nested {@code Map} (sub-tree). Renders at most 5 levels deep.

Blue ocean: decision logic is usually buried in code; this surfaces it as an auto-generated, always-current diagram.

params.name: branches
params.description: decision tree encoded as nested map

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.rendermachi.description:
Documents Erlang-style pattern matching: a set of patterns, the values they are matched against, and whether each match succeeds. Renders a three-column table with ✅ / ❌ match indicators.

Blue ocean: pattern-matching logic is tested but never documented — this surfaces the full match matrix as executable specification.

params.name: values
params.description: value strings tested against corresponding patterns

params.name: matches
params.description: true if the pattern matched, false if it did not

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.rendermachi.description:
Renders a 2-D ASCII heatmap using Unicode block characters (░▒▓█) for matrix data. Normalises values to [0, 1] and maps to four intensity levels. Ideal for correlation matrices, confusion matrices, and perf heat maps.

Blue ocean: 2-D visualisation in plain text — works in any terminal, any Markdown renderer, and any CI log.

params.name: title
params.description: descriptive title shown above the heatmap

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.render.Lazy.description: Get the cached value, computing it once if needed (thread-safe). After the first call, subsequent accesses return the cached value with no allocation overhead. The JIT compiler can inline this after warm-up. If the initializer returns {@code null} the result is still cached so the initializer is never called more than once.
io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.render.Lazy.returns: the cached value (may be {@code null} if the initializer returned null)

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.DtrTest#say.description:
Convenience method to create and render a cross-reference to another DocTest's section.

Creates a {@link DocTestRef} and delegates to {@link #sayRef(DocTestRef)}. The reference is rendered as a markdown link in Markdown mode or as a LaTeX cross-reference command in LaTeX mode. In LaTeX, the resolved section number (e.g., "Section 3.2") is automatically substituted after compilation.

params.name: docTestClass
params.description: the target DocTest class (must not be null)

params.name: anchor
params.description: the section/anchor name within that DocTest, e.g., "user-registration"               (typically derived from @DocSection annotation value)

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.rendermachi.description:
Analyzes a string and renders its structural profile using Java string APIs.

Blue ocean innovation: renders word count, line count, character category distribution, and Unicode metrics for any string — using only {@link String#chars()}, {@link String#lines()}, and {@link Character} APIs. Invaluable for documenting text processing and NLP APIs.

params.name: text
params.description: the string to profile (may be a sample payload, template, etc.)

Blue ocean: the first documentation primitive designed specifically for agentic AI workflows — no other documentation framework has this.

Finalizes all contained render machines concurrently using virtual threads.

This is where virtual threads provide the most measurable performance benefit: file I/O is blocking. A LaTeX compilation can take several seconds. Running all finalizations in parallel means the total wall-clock time is the slowest single finalizer, not the sum of all.

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.rendermachi.description:
Documents actor-model message passing: which actors send what messages to which targets. Renders a Mermaid sequence diagram showing concurrent asynchronous communication without shared state.

Blue ocean: Joe Armstrong's "share nothing" discipline made visible — every message hop is explicit, auditable, and always up to date.

params.name: actors
params.description: actor names in order of appearance

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.rendermachi.description: Record components schema — no-op in base class. */

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.DtrTest#say.description: Renders a TLDR (too long; didn't read) summary for blogs.

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.render.Lazy.description: Create a new lazy value with the given initializer.

params.name: initializer
params.description: function that computes the value on first access

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.assembly.In.description: Generates LaTeX index entries for all terms.

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.rendermachi.description:
Documents a Muda (waste) analysis: identifies the seven TPS wastes present in a process and the corresponding improvement actions. Renders a waste-type → description → action table and a waste-elimination ratio.

Blue ocean: waste is identified in retrospectives but rarely committed to documentation — this makes the analysis a first-class artefact.
Sets the output filename (typically the test class name).

params.name: fileName
params.description: the filename for the generated documentation

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.rendermachi.description:
Documents interface contract coverage across implementation classes. For each public method in the contract interface, checks whether each implementation provides a concrete override (✅ direct), inherits it (↗ inherited), or is missing it entirely (❌ MISSING). Uses only standard Java reflection.

If the contract is a sealed interface, permitted subclasses are automatically detected so the user does not need to enumerate them.

params.name: contract
params.description: the interface whose methods to verify

params.name: implementations
params.description: zero or more implementation classes to check

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.DtrTest#say.description: Documents interface contract coverage across implementation classes. */

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.rendermachi.description: Semantic API diff between two class versions — no-op in base class. */

io.github.seanchatmangpt.dtr.rendermachi.description: Auto-generates a Mermaid {@code classDiagram} from the given classes using reflection ({@link Class#getSuperclass()}, {@link Class#getInterfaces()}, {@link Class#getDeclaredMethods()}). Inheritance and implementation relationships are rendered as directed edges.

params.name: classes
params.description: the classes to include in the diagram
